title: Two-Stage Structural-Zero Gate For Pit Windows
summary: Most laps are obvious non-pit laps, so treating every row as the same binary problem may dilute the rare-event signal.
rationale: Most laps are obvious non-pit laps, so treating every row as the same binary problem may dilute the rare-event signal. Two-stage zero-inflated or hurdle formulations explicitly separate structural negatives from cases that are actually at risk.
implementation_hint: Stage 1 predicts `pit within next 3-5 laps` or `in pit window` with high recall; Stage 2 predicts `PitNextLap` only on gated candidate laps; final probability is `P(window) * P(next_lap | window)`, trained with strict OOF gating.
expected_effect: Can sharpen discrimination on the rare positives by removing easy negatives from the fine-grained model, often producing a complementary expert for blending.
risk: If the gate misses positives, Stage 2 cannot recover them; threshold choice and OOF discipline are critical.
